source.title: Proso Millet Cultivar Effects on Rheology of Dough and Quality Characteristics of Gluten-Free Breads

mapping_notes: Formula was 50% proso millet flour and 50% corn starch with 2% TIC 345 hydrocolloid blend on dry basis. Proprietary hydrocolloid blend is represented by Xanthan gum proxy for current model compatibility.

source.title: Effect of Additive Removal on the Physicochemical Properties of Gluten-Free Bread

mapping_notes: Commercial mix contains corn starch, rice flour, tapioca starch, salt, HPMC, psyllium, bamboo fiber, xanthan gum, pea protein, and preservatives; exact internal proportions are not disclosed, so it is mapped as an aggregate ingredient.

mapping_notes: Monocalcium phosphate removed; commercial mix remains aggregate-mapped because internal proportions are not disclosed.

mapping_notes: Monocalcium phosphate and sodium bicarbonate removed; commercial mix remains aggregate-mapped because internal proportions are not disclosed.

source.title: Effect of Additive Removal on the Physicochemical Properties of Gluten-Free Bread

mapping_notes: Monocalcium phosphate, sodium bicarbonate, and mono/diglycerides removed; commercial mix remains aggregate-mapped because internal proportions are not disclosed.

source.title: Effect of Hydration on Gluten-Free Breads Made with Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose in Comparison with Psyllium and Xanthan Gum

mapping_notes: Formula was 100 g rice flour plus 2 g HPMC per 100 g flour. Mapped formula is normalized over structural solids only; corrected hydration and quality endpoints are from Tables 1 and 2.

mapping_notes: Formula was 100 g rice flour plus 2 g psyllium husk fibre per 100 g flour. Mapped formula is normalized over structural solids only; corrected hydration and quality endpoints are from Tables 1 and 2.

mapping_notes: Formula was 100 g rice flour plus 2 g xanthan gum per 100 g flour. Mapped formula is normalized over structural solids only; corrected hydration and quality endpoints are from Tables 1 and 2.

mapping_notes: Formula was 100 g maize starch plus 2 g HPMC per 100 g starch. Maize starch is mapped to Corn starch; mapped formula is normalized over structural solids only. Corrected hydration and quality endpoints are from Tables 1 and 2.

source.title: Physico-chemical properties of an innovative gluten-free, low-carbohydrate and high protein-bread enriched with pea protein powder

mapping_notes: Control formulation (0% pea protein). Baker percentages per 100 g base flour (50 g buckwheat + 50 g flaxseed). Psyllium, potato fiber, and guar gum added as technological improvers. Mapped formula normalized over structural solids only; water (130 g), salt (2 g), and yeast (1 g) are stored as process variables. Volume from Table 1; hardness estimated from Figure 2a.

source.title: Physico-chemical properties of an innovative gluten-free, low-carbohydrate and high protein-bread enriched with pea protein powder

mapping_notes: 5% pea protein powder replacing base flour (47.5% buckwheat + 47.5% flaxseed). Volume from Table 1; hardness estimated from Figure 2a.

source.title: Physico-chemical properties of an innovative gluten-free, low-carbohydrate and high protein-bread enriched with pea protein powder

mapping_notes: 10% pea protein powder replacing base flour. Optimal level per paper: up to 10% PPP gave acceptable results without compromising texture. Volume from Table 1; hardness estimated from Figure 2a.

source.title: Physico-chemical properties of an innovative gluten-free, low-carbohydrate and high protein-bread enriched with pea protein powder

mapping_notes: 15% pea protein powder. Higher PPP levels resulted in noticeable volume reduction and increased hardness. Volume from Table 1; hardness estimated from Figure 2a.

source.title: Physico-chemical properties of an innovative gluten-free, low-carbohydrate and high protein-bread enriched with pea protein powder

mapping_notes: 20% pea protein powder. Significant increase in hardness and reduction in volume. Volume from Table 1; hardness estimated from Figure 2a.

source.title: Physico-chemical properties of an innovative gluten-free, low-carbohydrate and high protein-bread enriched with pea protein powder

mapping_notes: 25% pea protein powder. Highest protein level tested; most pronounced volume loss and firming. Volume from Table 1; hardness estimated from Figure 2a.

source.title: Impact of Raw, Roasted and Dehulled Chickpea Flours on Technological and Nutritional Characteristics of Gluten-Free Bread

mapping_notes: 25% of rice flour replaced with raw chickpea flour (CF). HPMC at 1.7 g/100 g flour. Water amount determined empirically to reach 125±5 BU (estimated ~85% hydration). Mapped formula normalized over structural solids: rice flour, chickpea flour, and HPMC. Chickpea processing type (raw) is not represented in the current ingredient model.

source.title: Impact of Raw, Roasted and Dehulled Chickpea Flours on Technological and Nutritional Characteristics of Gluten-Free Bread

mapping_notes: 25% of rice flour replaced with roasted chickpea flour (RCF). HPMC at 1.7 g/100 g flour. Water amount determined empirically to reach 125±5 BU (estimated ~85% hydration). Mapped formula normalized over structural solids. Roasting modifies starch gelatinization and protein functionality, but this processing difference is not represented in the current ingredient model.

source.title: Impact of Raw, Roasted and Dehulled Chickpea Flours on Technological and Nutritional Characteristics of Gluten-Free Bread

mapping_notes: 25% of rice flour replaced with dehulled chickpea flour (DCF). HPMC at 1.7 g/100 g flour. Water amount determined empirically to reach 125±5 BU (estimated ~85% hydration). Mapped formula normalized over structural solids. Dehulling removes the bran layer, reducing fiber content, but this processing difference is not represented in the current ingredient model.

source.title: Functional Optimization of a Novel Gluten-Free Bread Made with Tapioca Starch and Red Lentil Flour

mapping_notes: Control formulation with 100% MF (cornstarch:rice flour 28:25). Apple fiber (15g) not available in ingredient seed, omitted from mapped formula. Mapped formula renormalized to exclude apple fiber. Guar gum (3g) included.

source.title: Functional Optimization of a Novel Gluten-Free Bread Made with Tapioca Starch and Red Lentil Flour

mapping_notes: 70% MF, 30% red lentil, 0% tapioca. Red lentil flour mapped to Chickpea flour. Apple fiber omitted; renormalized.

source.title: Functional Optimization of a Novel Gluten-Free Bread Made with Tapioca Starch and Red Lentil Flour

mapping_notes: 40% MF, 30% red lentil, 30% tapioca starch. Red lentil flour mapped to Chickpea flour as closest legume proxy. Apple fiber (15g) omitted; mapped formula renormalized.

source.title: Functional Optimization of a Novel Gluten-Free Bread Made with Tapioca Starch and Red Lentil Flour

mapping_notes: 70% MF, 0% lentil, 30% tapioca starch. No legume fraction; pure starch blend. Apple fiber omitted; renormalized.

source.title: Functional Optimization of a Novel Gluten-Free Bread Made with Tapioca Starch and Red Lentil Flour

mapping_notes: 70% MF, 15% red lentil, 15% tapioca starch. Red lentil mapped to Chickpea flour. Apple fiber omitted; renormalized.

source.title: Functional Optimization of a Novel Gluten-Free Bread Made with Tapioca Starch and Red Lentil Flour

mapping_notes: 85% MF, 15% red lentil, no tapioca. Red lentil mapped to Chickpea flour. Apple fiber omitted; renormalized.

source.title: Functional Optimization of a Novel Gluten-Free Bread Made with Tapioca Starch and Red Lentil Flour

mapping_notes: 85% MF, no lentil, 15% tapioca starch. Pure starch blend with guar gum. Apple fiber omitted; renormalized.

source.title: Functional Optimization of a Novel Gluten-Free Bread Made with Tapioca Starch and Red Lentil Flour

mapping_notes: 55% MF, 30% red lentil, 15% tapioca. High lentil formulation. Red lentil mapped to Chickpea flour. Apple fiber omitted; renormalized.

source.title: Functional Optimization of a Novel Gluten-Free Bread Made with Tapioca Starch and Red Lentil Flour

mapping_notes: 55% MF, 15% red lentil, 30% tapioca. Optimal formulation region: lowest hardness (1.83 N). Red lentil mapped to Chickpea flour. Apple fiber omitted; renormalized.
